fact: February 21 sits exactly 52 days before the spring equinox in the Northern Hemisphere - that's like counting down 7 weeks and 3 days until day and night are perfectly equal!
In Antarctica, February 21 marks the peak of summer, when temperatures can actually reach a toasty 50°F (10°C) and penguins are busy molting their old feathers for shiny new ones.
February 21 is smack in the middle of maple syrup season in North America - the freeze-thaw cycles happening right now make tree sap flow like crazy!

People born on February 21 are right on the cusp between Aquarius and Pisces, making them some of the rarest zodiac combinations - they're called 'Aquarius-Pisces cuspers' and get traits from both signs.
In the Northern Hemisphere, sunrise happens about 2 minutes earlier each day around February 21, which means you're gaining almost 15 minutes of morning light each week!

February 21 marks when many deciduous trees in temperate climates start moving nutrients from their roots back up to their branches - even though you can't see it, they're secretly waking up from winter sleep.
Gross but cool: February 21 falls during prime 'mud season' in many northern regions, when frozen ground starts thawing and creates the sloppiest, stickiest, most boot-sucking mud of the entire year.
In Australia and New Zealand, February 21 is deep in autumn, and this is when many spiders start spinning extra-thick webs covered in morning dew that look like nature's own glittery decorations.
February 21 is the 52nd day of the year, which means there are exactly 313 days left until New Year's Eve - that's almost exactly 10 months and 10 days of adventures ahead!
Around February 21, the moon rises about 50 minutes later each night than the night before, creating some of the most dramatic moonrise timing changes of the entire year.

In northern lakes and ponds, February 21 marks when ice fishing season peaks because the ice is thickest, but underneath, fish are getting more active as they sense spring approaching through longer daylight filtering down.
